retry_reason: Failure only occurs on allocation (kzalloc_obj returns NULL). The refcount set itself never fails. Retrying allocation may succeed, but the wrapper does not implement any retry logic and callers typically propagate the error.
contract_summary: Creates and initializes a new live_active object, setting its initial kref reference count to 1 via kref_init. The caller receives ownership of a single reference and must call the appropriate release function (e.g., via i915_active release) to free the object. This is the creation point of the object's lifecycle; no additional references are acquired. The caller must check for NULL return to handle allocation failure.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to allocate a live_active struct and initialize its reference count via kref_init (a set operation). The function does nothing else besides setting up the i915_active; the only non-refcount logic is the allocation and a simple initialization call. The critical variable (the object) is returned to the caller.

check.reason: The kref_init call sets active->ref (via kref) to 1, and active is returned. The access path starts from the returned variable active, and the type chain matches the callee info. Segment count is consistent (4 segments, 4 types).
